title: Crispy Peppery Brazilian Beef Bowl
description: A sizzling bowl of peppery, crispy Brazilian-inspired beef served over quinoa and topped with crunchy almonds.
instructions: Rinse quinoa under cold water. Bring 2 cups water to a boil in a saucepan, add quinoa, reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer 15 minutes until liquid is absorbed. Fluff with a fork and set aside.

Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium‑high heat. Add the ground beef, season with salt, pepper, cumin, and paprika. Cook, breaking the meat apart with a spoon, until browned and crumbly, about 5 minutes. Remove beef from skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the remaining 1 tbsp oil. Add diced onion, bell pepper, and minced garlic. Sauté until vegetables soften, about 4–5 minutes.
Return browned beef to the skillet. Stir in soy sauce (or tamari) and lime juice, cooking for 2 minutes to combine flavors and slightly caramelize the mixture.
Add the cooked quinoa to the skillet, stirring to evenly distribute the grains. Cook for an additional 2 minutes, allowing the bowl to heat through and the flavors to meld.
Taste the mixture and adjust seasoning with additional salt, pepper, or lime if desired.
Divide the warm beef‑quinoa mixture evenly into four bowls.
Sprinkle 1 tbsp crushed almonds over each bowl for a crunchy, crispy finish.
(Optional) Garnish with chopped cilantro and serve with extra lime wedges on the side.
Serve hot as a satisfying dinner that feels both comforting and romantic.
